title: As Trump Pushes Deportations, a Skyrocketing Caseload Strains Immigration Courts
summary: A federal surge has more than doubled caseloads within some immigration courts nationwide. Lawyers say the tactic is causing errors and confusion.

title: Paxton’s Senate Bid Raises the Stakes in His War on Latino Voting Groups
summary: The Texas attorney general has mounted an all-out effort to prove Democratic Hispanic groups have been corrupting elections. Now he could be the beneficiary of his own attacks.

title: ICE Says Detainees Are ‘Worst of the Worst.’ Government Data Disagrees.
summary: Federal officials said they are removing killers and rapists from the streets. Data obtained by The New York Times indicates most detainees at a Newark facility haven’t been convicted of crimes.

title: Trump’s Defense Department Sees Growing Espionage Threat From Israel
summary: The Defense Department has increased the counterintelligence threat assessment to its highest level, and Israel is believed to have eavesdropped on American negotiations with Iran.

title: What Visual Evidence Tells Us About Israel’s Use of White Phosphorus in Lebanon
summary: Videos collected by The Times shows how the Israeli military has deployed a munition that can be extremely harmful over populated areas in Lebanon.

title: Israeli Strike Kills 3 Lebanese Soldiers, Days After Truce Was Signed
summary: Israel invaded Lebanon and occupied parts of the country to fight Hezbollah, an Iran-backed armed group, but its military offensive has drawn in others.

title: Infant Killed as Israeli Military Fires on Car in West Bank, Palestinian Officials Say
summary: Israel’s military said a soldier opened fire after Israeli troops “perceived a vehicle accelerating toward them.” The baby’s grandmother, who was in the car, disputed that account.

title: The Bidens Return With New Book and South Dakota Speech Targeting Trump
summary: The re-emergence of the former first family has been fraught for some Democrats who just want to move on.

title: Graham Platner Says His Past Is Being ‘Weaponized’ Amid Growing Democratic Concern
summary: The presumptive Democratic Senate nominee in Maine said the state would have his back in the face of accusations he has denied.

title: What Are Democrats’ Best Senate Pickup Opportunities?
summary: We’ve grouped six battleground states by some of the factors that shape how likely it is for the party to flip each Senate seat.

title: A.I. Companies Don’t Know What to Do With Alex Bores
summary: Outside groups have spent roughly $12 million to support or oppose Mr. Bores’s campaign for a House seat in Manhattan, elevating his name in a crowded race.

title: Trump Greets Farmers in Wisconsin, but Says He Could Be Home Watching TV
summary: President Trump was in Wisconsin to reassure farmers who have been stung by his tariff policies and rising fuel prices from the war in Iran.

title: U.S. Job Market Pushes Past Shocks and Strains
summary: Employers added 172,000 jobs in May, adding to a vigorous pace of hiring in recent months. But wage growth is not keeping up with higher prices and consumers remain pessimistic.

title: Trump Pardons Stephen Buyer, Former Congressman Convicted of Insider Trading
summary: Stephen Buyer, a former Republican representative from Indiana, was convicted of trading stock related to two deals before they were made public.

title: New York City’s Most Patient Soccer Fans Are Ready for the Party
summary: Uzbekistan is new to the World Cup. Norway and Haiti haven’t been there in decades. For expat communities all over the city, the wait is nearly over.

title: U.S. Amputee World Cup Contenders Are Fierce on Crutches
summary: With the Amputee Football World Cup nearing, an elite squad of athletes gathered on Long Island to vie for a spot on the national men’s team, displaying skill and grit.

title: As Ebola Spreads in East Africa, Will China Step Up?
summary: China is well positioned to help stop the deadly virus, and could move into a gap left by U.S. retreat.

title: Raphael Warnock Says the Supreme Court Has Done ‘Violence’ to Democracy
summary: The Democrat from Georgia on what he sees as the moral issues of our time.

title: The States Where Life Is Better
summary: Trust, well-being and mental health are all down in America. But some states are better to live in than others, according to a new study.

title: Graham Platner and the Rise of the ‘Dirtbag’ Democrat
summary: And what the Maine candidate reveals about politics today.

title: Balancing Act
summary: The tension between sticking to what we know and seeking out new experiences is one of comfort versus novelty. How can we have both?
